channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden campaign spotlights massive June fundraising haul in 2024 election rematch with Trump
item.description: President Biden's 2024 re-election campaign and the Democratic National Committee brought in over a quarter of a billion dollars in fundraising the past three months
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the blockbuster Supreme Court presidential immunity ruling impacts the Biden-Trump 2024 rematch
item.description: Democrats and Republicans weigh in on how will the blockbuster Supreme Court ruling on presidential immunity impact the Joe Biden-Donald Trump 2024 election rematch
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dems who were pressed to retire due to age concerns have a history of refusing to go
item.description: President Biden's apparent determination to stick to his re-election bid is in line with other elderly Democrat politicians like Nancy Pelosi and the late Dianne Feinstein.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York ripe for Trump’s taking, GOP chair says: Biden is in a 1980-Carter moment
item.description: New Yorkers will judge President Biden and former President Trump similarly to how they judged Jimmy Carter and Ronald Reagan in 1980, NYS GOP Chairman Ed Cox said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Highlights from Supreme Court term: Rulings on Trump, regulation, abortion, guns and homelessness
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court ended its term by ruling for the first time that former presidents have broad immunity from prosecution, a decision that almost certainly means Donald Trump won't stand trial before the November election.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge sides with 16 states, putting on pause Biden's delay of consideration of gas export projects
item.description: LAKE CHARLES, La. (AP) — The Biden administration can’t delay consideration of projects aimed at exporting liquefied natural gas while a legal challenge by 16 Republican-led states plays out in federal court, a Louisiana judge said Monday. U.S. District Judge James Cain, Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After Supreme Court immunity ruling, Biden draws sharp contrast with Trump on obeying rule of law
item.description: President Joe Biden warns that a Supreme Court ruling granting presidents broad immunity from prosecution would make an unchecked Republican Donald Trump “more emboldened to do whatever he wants” if he regains the White House in November’s election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: After Supreme Court immunity ruling, Biden draws sharp contrast with Trump on obeying rule of law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden warned Monday that a Supreme Court ruling granting presidents broad immunity from prosecution would make an unchecked Republican Donald Trump “more emboldened to do whatever he wants” if he regains the White House in November’s election.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the Supreme Court immunity ruling in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court's ruling Monday in former President Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case makes it all but certain that the Republican will not face trial in Washington ahead of the November election.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden: Supreme Court immunity decision sets ‘dangerous precedent’
item.description: President Joe Biden called the Supreme Court's decision an "attack" on "long-established" American principles.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: US to pay for flights to help Panama remove migrants who may be heading north
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is going to pay for flights and offer other help to Panama to remove migrants under an agreement signed Monday, as the Central American country's new president has vowed to shut down the treacherous Darien Gap used by people traveling north to the United States.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Spartz charged with weapons violation at Dulles Airport
item.description: Rep. Victoria Spartz (R-Ind.) was charged with a weapons violation last week after she brought a handgun to Washington Dulles International Airport in Virginia, authorities and Spartz's office say.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he can end the Russia-Ukraine war in one day. Russia's UN ambassador says he can't
item.description: Donald Trump has said repeatedly he can settle the war between Russia and Ukraine in one day if he’s elected president again.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Project 2025: What is in conservative groups' plan if Trump wins?
item.description: A nearly 1,000-page handbook by conservative groups outlines how Trump could dismantle federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: AOC intends to file articles of impeachment after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez says she intends to file articles of impeachment after the Supreme Court ruling in former President Donald Trump’s immunity case.
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Charboneau, NewsNation

item.title: 7 Democrats being floated as potential Biden replacements
item.description: President Biden’s campaign is intensely trying to quell speculation that he may drop out of the 2024 race following his lackluster debate performance last week.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9

item.title: Restaurants, bars carved out last minute from California's ban on hidden fees
item.description: In a last-minute change, Gov. Gavin Newsom signed a bill on Saturday to exempt restaurants, bars and caterers from California's new law banning "hidden fees" on goods and services in the state.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Nicole Mitchell seeks dismissal of burglary charge while calling for release of more evidence
item.description: DFL Sen. Nicole Mitchell of Woodbury was arrested and charged with burglary in April when she was found in the basement of her stepmother’s home in Detroit Lakes. Her next court hearing is now scheduled for July 9.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Analysts on presidential debate and recent Supreme Court moves
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis talks with legal and political analysts about the fallout from Thursday’s presidential debate and the significance of the newest Supreme Court decisions.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis, MPR News

item.title: Court allows key part of Biden student loan relief plan to resume
item.description: A federal appeals court on Sunday ruled that a key part of President Joe Biden’s student loan forgiveness plan can resume after an injunction was placed on the initiative.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas, Fox 5

item.title: Sotomayor scolds immunity decision for making presidents ‘king above the law’
item.description: Justice Sonia Sotomayor said the Supreme Court’s decision granting former President Trump immunity for official acts “completely insulate[s] presidents from criminal liability” in a forceful dissent issued Monday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Texas sets execution date for East Texas man accused in shaken baby case
item.description: Lawyers for Robert Robertson say the science used to sentence him to death is questionable and that prior health issues could explain the death of his 2-year-old daughter in 2003.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: The Republicans who want to be Trump's VP were once harsh critics with key policy differences
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s hard to refer to someone as “Hitler” and end up in their good graces, let alone potentially become the person they choose to help lead the country. But Ohio Sen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-presidents have some immunity, sends Trump case back to lower court
item.description: In a historic 6-3 ruling, the justices said for the first time that former presidents have absolute immunity from prosecution for their official acts and no immunity for unofficial acts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: SCOTUS sends Trump immunity case back to lower court but issues ruling on presidential immunity
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday extended the delay in the Washington criminal case against Donald Trump on charges he plotted to overturn his 2020 presidential election loss, all but ending prospects the former president could be tried before the November election.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court sends Trump immunity case back to lower court, dimming chance of trial before election
item.description: The Supreme Court has extended the delay in the Washington criminal case against Donald Trump on charges he plotted to overturn his 2020 presidential election loss, all but ending prospects he could be tried before the November election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: How the Summer EBT program will help struggling NY families
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York State began accepting applications Monday for a new program designed to help families with school-aged children buy groceries this summer. Families in the Summer EBT program will receive a one-time installment of $120 per child for the summer.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Live updates: Supreme Court rules Trump has presumptive criminal immunity for official acts
item.description: In a monumental decision Monday, the Supreme Court ruled that former presidents enjoy a presumption of criminal immunity for official acts while in the White House, handing a win to former President Trump. The move is likely to delay Trump's federal election subversion case in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams on how $112.4 billion budget will shape future of NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Monday is the first day of the new fiscal year for New York City and the ink is still drying on the record-breaking $112.4 billion budget deal signed by the City Council.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Some student loan borrowers have July payment waived: Here's why
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Millions of student loan borrowers will not have to make debt payments this month. About 3 million borrowers will have both their July payment and interest charges waived.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump immunity decision in balance as Supreme Court caps term
item.description: The Supreme Court is set to rule on Monday whether former President Trump has immunity from criminal prosecution, a monumental decision that comes just days after the court dropped a decision throwing into doubt charges against hundreds of Jan. 6 rioters and Trump himself.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: George Latimer sets his sights on the NY-16 general election
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Westchester County Executive George Latimer defeated incumbent Rep. Jamaal Bowman with 58 percent of the vote on Tuesday in the NY-16 Democratic primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Biden to discuss future of re-election campaign with family on Sunday
item.description: Biden’s trip to Camp David was planned before Thursday’s debate. He and first lady Jill Biden are scheduled to join their children and grandchildren there late Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Carol E. Lee, Kristen Welker, Jonathan Allen, Mike Memoli and Monica Alba, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How replacing Biden as Democratic nominee would actually work
item.description: Replacing President Biden as the Democratic nominee would be highly complicated, and likely impossible, unless the president voluntarily decided to back out on his own.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Did Trump actually call veterans who died in wars 'suckers'?
item.description: Near the end of the first 30 minutes, Biden claimed Trump previously verbally attacked and disrespected American veterans, particularly ones who were injured or died during combat.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WGN 9

item.title: Trump and Biden mix it up over policy and each other in a debate that turns deeply personal
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — They passed on a handshake at the start, and from there President Joe Biden and former President Donald Trump went right to mixing it up on policy — and each other — in their first 2024 presidential debate on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Connecticut Gov. Ned Lamont tests positive for COVID-19
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (PIX11) -- Connecticut Gov. Ned Lamont has tested positive for COVID-19, a spokesperson said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11
